evidence.scientific_clarity: All brief requirements met: 12 studies with CI lines, weight-proportional markers, red diamond for pooled estimate (0.245 [0.196, 0.294]), dashed null line at 0, numeric annotation column on right.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set in gen_figure.py; PDF uses Type3 DejaVuSans (not embedded TrueType); SVG stores text as glyph path outlines via <use> elements (not selectable); default DejaVu Sans font used throughout.
evidence.palette: Only two data-encoding colors (#1f77b4 blue for studies, #d62728 red for pooled diamond); shape also distinguishes them (circle vs diamond); no green/red confusion since green is absent, but red is used for pooled which is fine directionally.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(8,6) with 13 rows fits reasonably; annotation column added on right; separator line between studies and pooled; minor issue: y-range has ~0.7 units of blank space below pooled diamond.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and pooled values; no legend needed (shape+color self-explanatory); title is present; annotation column duplicates CI info already shown graphically, which is standard for forest plots.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains real BT/TJ text operators (text is selectable) and vector paths (bezier curves for markers); however fonts are Type3 (not TrueType/fonttype=42), meaning glyphs are outlines rather than embedded font program — partial vector fidelity.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (27949 bytes), figure.png (81532 bytes), figure.svg (81234 bytes), caption.tex (394 bytes), gen_figure.py (2847 bytes) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states what is shown (log ORs, 95% CIs, 12 studies), explains marker sizing (inverse-variance weight), gives specific numerical finding (pooled 0.245; 95% CI [0.196, 0.294]), and describes method (inverse-variance weighting).

overall_strengths: Complete forest plot implementation: all brief elements present (CI lines, weight-proportional squares, pooled diamond, null line, numeric annotation column), with correct inverse-variance pooling math.
Caption is publication-quality: includes specific numerical claim, explains visual encoding (marker size = weight), and matches figure values exactly.
overall_weaknesses: Typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' not set — PDF uses Type3 DejaVuSans outlines instead of embedded TrueType, and SVG text is stored as glyph path outlines rather than selectable <text> elements; default DejaVu Sans font not replaced with a journal-preferred face.

Minor layout: small gap below pooled diamond (~0.7 y-units of dead space), and the annotation column at fontsize=7.5 may be too small for print; no explicit weight column or heterogeneity statistic (I², Q-test) which reviewers often expect.
